mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20d forward returns are negative in >55% of rate-cut months over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off selling and negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Positive 5d returns occur in >60% of instances where UNRATE rises >0.2pp.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based domestic demand recovery, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST yields negative 60d returns in >50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect strengthening manufacturing capacity utilization, which translates to higher corporate earnings expectations and positive equity returns over one month.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases of >1% fail to produce positive 20d returns in >55% of observations during early-cycle periods.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation erodes real corporate margins and prompts expectations of tighter monetary policy, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL month-over-month increases >0.3% are followed by positive 5d returns in >65% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth surpassing 2.5% confirms robust economic expansion, supporting sustained corporate profit growth and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60d forward returns are negative in >50% of quarters where GDPC1 growth exceeds 2.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate signals a tightening labor market that boosts consumer spending and corporate revenues, yielding positive equity returns over one month.
failure_condition: The joint condition of PAYEMS growth >100k and UNRATE decline produces negative 20d returns in >55% of months.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle signals demand destruction from higher borrowing costs, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening cycles are followed by positive 5d returns in >60% of cases.
